COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-24-2023 Continued To Jul-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 318
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Sixup PBC, Inc., A Delaware Corporation.

Dept. 318. Judge: Christopher C. Hite. Clerk: K. Colobong/L. Bareng/K. Valdes. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:48 a.m.

George Lee, Esq., appeared for plaintiff MV Cal, LLC A California Limited Liability. James To, Property manager and Custodian of Record was also present, placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Sixup PBC, Inc.

Plaintiff's Complaint for DAMAGES FOR: BREACH OF CONTRACT, CIV. CODE 1951.2; and COMMON COUNTS of Contract was filed on March 22, 2022.

Defendant Sixup PBC, Inc's Answer to complaint was filed on June 1, 2022.

Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on August 17, 2022, setting Trial for March 20, 2023.

Notice of continuance of Trial was filed on February 16, 2023, setting Trial for July 24, 2023, in Department 206.

Assigned to Department 318 on July 24, 2023, for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up Hearing.
Doe defendants have NOT been dismissed.

The Court continues the matter to July 31, 2023, in Department 318 at 9:00 a.m. Plaintiff was instructed to provide Notice to Defendant with a new Court Trial date and to submit to the Court the following:

1. Trial Brief
2. Statement of Damages
3. Proposed Judgment

Proceedings concluded at 11:01 a.m.

Motion To Compel Deposition Of Deft Pbc, Inc. And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
George Lee, Esq. 415-979-0500 Seiler Epstein LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTY AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. Mr. Lee seeks clarification on proposed order to be submitted. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MV CAL, LLC's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Pbc, Inc. And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion appears to be procedurally proper. Plaintiff gave notice to Defendant of the taking of Defendant's deposition and Defendant failed to appear at the noticed time and date and failed to propose alternative dates for its deposition to occur. Defendant has not filed an opposition to the motion to explain the failure to appear at the noticed deposition. Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450(c)(1) mandates the imposition of monetary sanctions under the circumstances presented, unless the court find that the person subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. No such circumstances appear here, and the amount of sanctions that Defendants request appears reasonable. The Court intends to grant Plaintiff's motion and issue the Proposed Order in the form that Plaintiff provided with an award of $2310. Plaintiff should submit a form of order in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem at tim@ncalegal.com.

Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
